authors.title: ERP CORE
authors.description: ERP CORE is a comprehensive open-access resource providing raw EEG data from 40 neurotypical participants performing six standardized task paradigms designed to elicit seven widely-used event-related potential (ERP) components: N170, mismatch negativity, N2pc, N400, P3, lateralized readiness potential, and error-related negativity. The dataset includes optimized experimental scripts, stimulus materials, demographic information, and data processing pipelines to facilitate reproducible ERP research and component characterization.
authors.methods_description: EEG data were collected from 40 neurotypical adults performing six task paradigms in a single recording session: an Eriksen flanker task (ERN/LRP), passive auditory oddball (MMN), face perception task (N170), visual search task (N2pc), word pair judgment task (N400), and active visual oddball (P3). EEG coordinates are defined using EEGLAB orientation (Anterior-Left-Superior).
